summary: boardflow preinstall hook downloads and executes remote Windows binary from pooron.org
details: package.json declares `"preinstall": "node install.js"`, which fires automatically on `npm install boardflow`. install.js is heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io-style `_0x...` symbols and split string array). The reconstructed string table contains the literal URL fragments that concatenate to `https://www.pooron.org/ice.exe`, plus `/tmp`, a `tester_` filename prefix, `.exe`, `755`, `win32`, `darwin`, `open`, `child_process`, `randomBytes`, and per-platform `spawn(..., {detached:true, stdio:'ignore'})` branches. The script downloads the remote executable to a randomized path under TEMP, `chmodSync(..., 0o755)`, and spawns it detached on Windows, macOS, and Linux. Exceptions are swallowed to hide failures. The fetched binary is hosted on a third-party domain unrelated to the package's stated kanban-board purpose, is unpinned and unverified, and runs with the installer's privileges before any application code is loaded.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
